intro: Utopia makes it easier to explore tours, maps and cabins in one place, with inspiration and useful information for trip planning.

privacyModal.title: Privacy Statement
privacyModal.intro: Utopia is developed as a <1>student project</1> at the University of South-Eastern Norway (USN). Information stored in the solution is used only to demonstrate how the application works in an educational context.
privacyModal.s1Body: The solution may store information that you create or enter yourself, such as user profile, tours, activities and other content relevant to using the application.
privacyModal.s2Title: 2. What is the information used for?
privacyModal.s2Body: The information is used only to display and test features in the system. It is not shared as part of commercial operations and is not used for marketing or further distribution.
privacyModal.s3Body: Data entered into the solution is intended for the project period and may be removed when the school project ends or when it is no longer needed.
privacyModal.s4Body: If you want content or user data to be removed, you can contact the student group behind the project.

termsModal.title: Terms of Use
termsModal.intro: By using Utopia you accept that the solution is a <1>student project</1> developed as part of teaching at USN. The service is made for learning, demonstration and testing.
termsModal.s1Body: The application should be used responsibly. Content and features are designed to show how a hiking platform can be built, not as a finished commercial service.
termsModal.s2Body: The student group makes no guarantee that the solution is always available or error-free. Users should therefore avoid storing sensitive or critical information in the system.
termsModal.s3Body: It is not permitted to attempt to damage the solution, bypass security, upload inappropriate content or use the service in a way that may negatively affect other users.
termsModal.s4Body: Content, features and text may be adjusted during the project as part of further development and testing.
